title: Context Hub Onboarding Tour
description: Learn how Context Hub provides AI agents with curated, versioned API documentation through a CLI tool and content registry
prerequisites: Basic Node.js and ES modules knowledge

steps.title: Welcome to Context Hub
steps.description: Context Hub (chub) is a CLI tool and registry that bridges the gap between rapidly evolving APIs and LLM knowledge cutoffs. It gives AI coding agents access to accurate, up-to-date API documentation instead of relying on training data. The project has two main parts: the CLI application in cli and the content registry in content with 68 providers and 115 docs.

steps.title: CLI Entry Points & Commands
steps.description: The CLI is built with commander. Two entry points exist: chub for the main CLI and chub-mcp for the MCP server. The highlighted index.js file is the orchestration hub that registers all commands. The commands directory contains seven handlers for search, get, annotate, feedback, update, cache, and build.

interactiveActions.description: Click to view the CLI orchestration hub

steps.title: Core Library Modules
steps.description: The lib directory contains the engine that powers everything. registry.js handles loading, merging, and searching entries across multiple sources. bm25.js implements full-text search ranking. cache.js manages local caching and doc fetching. config.js parses the multi-source configuration. These modules work together to provide fast, reliable documentation access.

steps.title: Content Registry
steps.description: The content directory is the heart of the project — it stores curated API documentation from 68 providers. Each provider has a docs subdirectory with entries. Docs use YAML frontmatter for metadata and support multi-language variants (javascript, python, ts subdirectories). The build command compiles this into a searchable registry.json.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over provider directories to see their docs

steps.title: MCP Server Integration
steps.description: Context Hub exposes its capabilities as MCP tools, letting AI agents like Claude Code and Cursor access docs directly. server.js sets up the MCP server with stdio transport, and tools.js implements five tools: chub_search, chub_get, chub_list, chub_annotate, and chub_feedback. This is how agents fetch accurate API docs during coding.

steps.title: Testing & CI/CD
steps.description: Tests use vitest and live in two directories: cli/test (legacy) and cli/tests (current). CI runs on GitHub Actions testing against Node 18, 20, and 22. The deploy-content workflow builds and publishes the registry to CDN, while publish.yml handles npm releases of the aisuite/chub package.

steps.title: Project Documentation
steps.description: The docs directory contains guides for contributors and users. design.md explains architectural decisions. content-guide.md shows how to add new provider docs. cli-reference.md is the full command reference. The byod-guide and private-content docs cover bringing your own documentation and using internal/private content sources.

steps.title: The Complete Picture
steps.description: You've now seen the full Context Hub architecture: the CLI in cli orchestrates commands, lib modules provide search and caching, the content registry in content holds 68 providers of API docs, MCP integration lets agents access everything programmatically, and comprehensive tests and CI keep it all reliable. To get started contributing, check content-guide.md in docs for adding docs or dive into cli/src for CLI improvements.
